title: Module 1
content:
Basics of Measurement: Generalized block diagram of Instrumentation
system, Standards, Calibration of meters, Static and Dynamic characteristics
of transducers, Errors in measurements and its analysis.
Measurement of resistance, capacitance and inductance: DC and AC bridges,
Sources and detectors- Balance Equation, Wheatstone Bridge, Kelvin’s
Double bridge, Maxwell’s inductance bridge and Maxwell’s inductance
capacitance bridge, Schering bridge, Wien’s bridge.

title: Module 2
content:
Transducers - Classification of transducers, Factors influencing choice of
transducer. Passive transducers: Principle of operation, Construction details,
characteristics, types, measuring circuits and applications of Resistance
transducers: Potentiometer, Strain gauge (gauge factor derivation)- Strain
gauge Load Cell-Resistance thermometer (2 wire,3 wire & 4 wire)-
Thermistor. Inductive Transducers: LVDT. Capacitive transducers: Variable
air gap, Variable Area, Variable permittivity.

title: Module 3
content:
Active transducers: Principle of operation,
Construction details,
Characteristics, types and Applications of Thermocouple (cold junction
compensation), Piezo electric transducers, Hall effect transducers,
Photovoltaic Cell.
Opto-electric Transducers: Photodiode, Phototransistor, Photoconductive
cell, LDR
Accelerometers: Piezoelectric, potentiometric, LVDT accelerometer.
Non-Contact type transducers: Infrared-Ultrasonic proximity sensors.
Microwave Level Switches, Optical level sensor, Ultrasonic level sensor.
Pyrometers: Optical, Radiation pyrometer.

title: Module 4
content:
Measuring, Display, Analysing & Recording Instruments: Working and
Torque equations of PMMC, PMMI, Electrodynamometer. Sampling
Oscilloscope, Digital Storage oscilloscopes, LCD displays, Spectrum
Analyzer, Instrument Transformers: Current Transformer- Potential
Transformer. Peak response voltmeter- True RMS meter- Q meter-Strip
chart recorder-XY Plotter.
Suggestion on Project Topics
Note:
1.The project proposal submitted by each group (4 to 8 students) must include a measurement system,
which can be either analog or digital.
2.The sensor calibration is mandatory.
3. Students can incorporate suitable signal conditioning circuits, communication standards or
display   devices in the project work. They can evaluate the outputs and run necessary analytics on the
data generated.
4.Sample projects:
a. Implementation of a temperature transducer for low temperature measurement
b.  Wind measurement system using anemometers
c.  Vibration measurement using accelerometers
